definition: A Promise is a JavaScript object representing the eventual result of an asynchronous operation, moving from pending to either fulfilled (with a value) or rejected (with an error). async/await is syntax that lets you write Promise-based code in a sequential, readable style.
layman: A Promise is an IOU for a value that isn't ready yet. async/await lets you 'wait' for that IOU as if the code were normal step-by-step code, without freezing everything else.
content:
## Async/await & Promises

JavaScript is single-threaded and **non-blocking**: slow work (network, timers) runs in the background and resolves later via Promises, so the program stays responsive.

### Promise states

A Promise is **pending**, then settles once as either **fulfilled** (has a value) or **rejected** (has an error). It never changes again.

```js
fetch(url)
  .then(res => res.json())   // runs on fulfill
  .catch(err => log(err))    // runs on reject
  .finally(() => done())     // runs either way
```

### async/await

`await` pauses inside an `async` function until a Promise settles, then yields its value (or throws on rejection). Errors use ordinary `try/catch`:

```ts
async function ask(prompt: string): Promise<string> {
  try {
    const res = await callModel(prompt)   // waits, doesn't block other work
    return res.text
  } catch (err) {
    throw new Error("model call failed")
  }
}
```

An `async` function always returns a `Promise`.

### Sequential vs parallel

`await`ing in a loop runs calls **one after another**. To run them **concurrently**, start them together and await `Promise.all`:

| Combinator | Behavior |
|------------|----------|
| `Promise.all` | all succeed, or reject on first failure |
| `Promise.allSettled` | wait for all, get each result/error |
| `Promise.race` | settle with the first to finish (great for timeouts) |
| `Promise.any` | first to *fulfill* (ignores rejections until all fail) |

### Why this matters for Gen AI

Every model/API/vector-store call is async. You `await` completions, run many embedding requests in parallel with `Promise.all`, add timeouts with `Promise.race`, and use `try/catch` to handle rate limits and transient failures — all without blocking the app.
keyPoints: A Promise is pending then settles once as fulfilled (value) or rejected (error).
async functions return Promises; await unwraps a Promise's value or throws on rejection.
Handle async errors with try/catch (await) or .catch (chaining).
await in a loop is sequential; use Promise.all to run calls in parallel.
Promise.race/any/allSettled cover timeouts, first-success and collect-all patterns.
